Fix SBOM summary table: use HTML <code> tags instead of markdown backticks

The addTable() API generates HTML <table> elements where markdown
syntax is not rendered. Backticks were showing as literal characters
in the job summary. Switched to <code> HTML tags for proper inline
code formatting of file paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: multi-channel notification system with QR integrations

Implements full multi-channel notification infrastructure across DB,
backend, and frontend — closing the gap from email-only (60%) to a
complete system supporting in-app, SMS, push, WhatsApp, Slack, Teams,
and Google Meet.

## DB (migration 0015)
- Extends notification_channel enum: SMS, IN_APP, WHATSAPP, SLACK, TEAMS
- Creates notification_preferences (quiet hours, digest mode, per-channel toggles)
- Creates in_app_notifications (with unread index)
- Creates device_tokens (FCM push, per platform)
- Creates connected_integrations (nonce QR, OAuth tokens AES-256-GCM encrypted)
- Adds phone_number column to unified_profiles

## Backend (notification_service)
- HTTP server on port 3012 (Axum) + Kafka consumer run concurrently
- 18 API routes: notifications CRUD, preferences upsert, device tokens,
  WhatsApp/Slack/Teams/Google OAuth and webhook flows
- Fanout dispatchers: in_app, SMS (Twilio), push (FCM), WhatsApp, Slack,
  Teams Adaptive Cards — all gated on notification_preferences + quiet hours
- 5 new Kafka topic subscriptions: EscrowRelease, PayoutVeto, MatchResult,
  WarrantyClaimed, MentorshipPaired
- AES-256-GCM encryption for OAuth access/refresh tokens at rest

## Frontend
- notifications/page.tsx: loads from API on mount, 30s unread count polling,
  optimistic mark-read, graceful fallback to demo data
- notification-settings/page.tsx: loads/saves preferences via API, new
  Connected Integrations panel with QR codes (WhatsApp, Slack, Google Meet)
  and webhook URL paste (Teams); polls integration status every 5s
- api.ts: InAppNotification, NotifPrefs, IntegrationStatus types +
  fetchInAppNotifications, fetchUnreadCount, markNotificationRead,
  markAllNotificationsRead, fetchNotificationPreferences,
  saveNotificationPreferences, registerDeviceToken, fetchIntegrationsStatus,
  initWhatsAppConnect, saveTeamsWebhook, disconnectIntegration
- next.config.ts: proxy rewrites for /api/notifications, /api/notification-preferences,
  /api/device-tokens, /api/integrations → localhost:3012
- docker-compose.yml: port 3012 exposed + 12 new env vars (Twilio, FCM,
  Slack, Google, encryption key) with dev_placeholder defaults
- docs/secret-registry.md: 10 new secrets registered
- .gitignore: add .env.local and .env.*.local patterns
- .claude/launch.json: autoPort: true for dev server

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

to CWD with kebab-case names derived from the first # Heading.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix pipeline first-stage: remove repeat-visit false positives; fix JSON save

Repeat-visit bonus removed from _simple_first_stage_scoring:
  Earth-crossing asteroids naturally appear dozens of times in a 200-year
  CAD window (Apophis ~50x, Eros ~60x). Without real orbital periods to
  compare expected vs actual return frequency, N>=3 visits is not
  anomalous — it is expected gravitational dynamics for short-period NEAs.
  Result: previously all 27,632 objects passed the first-stage gate.
  Now only distance (<0.05 AU) and velocity (<8 km/s) trigger first-stage.
  Repeat-visit analysis is correctly placed at BC11 population level (Option 8).
  n_visits is still computed and stored in signal_breakdown for auditability.

JSON serialization fixed in _save_pipeline_results:
  validated_candidates contain datetime fields; json.dump raised
  TypeError. Added default=_default handler: datetime.isoformat() for
  date objects, str() fallback for everything else.

commit.message:
fix: allow unauthenticated requests when BV_API_KEY is set

Previously, setting BV_API_KEY caused all requests without a bearer
token to be rejected with 401, blocking public users entirely.
Now missing tokens are allowed through as unauthenticated (rate-limited),
while only invalid tokens get 401. This lets public users access the
server with rate limits while authenticated operators bypass them.

update: competitive pricing + NDS-039 Not For Resale label

Pricing strategy:
- PAL games ~10% under retronintendokopen.nl
- USA/NTSC imports priced HIGHER than PAL (rarer in Europe)
- Competitor sells mostly German versions, our PAL originals
  are worth more

Price changes:
- HeartGold PAL CIB: 337.95 → 179.95 (redelijke staat)
- Explorers of Sky PAL CIB: 208.95 → 104.95
- Black 2 PAL CIB: 191.95 → 139.95
- Black PAL CIB: 168.95 → 109.95
- Diamond PAL CIB: 185.95 → 169.95
- Platinum PAL loose: 138.95 → 114.95

Add back buttons at all flow stages and video attribution caption

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM to prevent "server failed" status (v1.1.2)

Claude Code sends SIGINT then SIGTERM to kill MCP server processes.
Without a handler, the process dies from the signal (non-zero exit),
which Claude Code counts as a failure. Add signal handlers that raise
SystemExit(0) for a clean exit.

repository.description: Modeling the Keep Your Pay Act from Cory Booker

commit.message:
Fix tile blocking: check all higher layers, not just layer+1

A tile at layer 0 was incorrectly considered free even when a tile at
layer 2 or 3 sat visually on top of it (because only layer+1 was
checked). This caused unwinnable end-game states where the last pair
had one tile nearly invisible under another. Now any higher-layer tile
at the same position blocks the tile below.
